channel.title: golang on Diego J.'s Software Tar Pit
channel.description: Recent content in golang on Diego J.'s Software Tar Pit

item.title: Using LLMs on an unknown domain
item.author: diegojromerolopez@gmail.com (Diego J. Romero-López)
item.description: Using LLMs on an unknown domain We often hear that Large Language Models (LLMs) empower engineers by augmenting their existing expertise. But what happens when the engineer is a complete novice in the domain? Can a LLM be more than just a powerful autocomplete? Can it be a partner when developing for a domain you have never touched? Introduction I have been “vibe coding” extensively for the last six months. My background is primarily in web technologies, but I decided to push the limits: build a desktop application in a language I have played around but don’t consider my “forte”.
